items.Patch_Note: Source-verified dose update; copy over existing row or compare before paste

Replaces vague 1–2 tsp with age-band dosing
New procedural/triage row; not routine prescription
Conservative row; medication use limited by age/safety
New disease row; source supports oral steroid within 72h age ≥16, but oral prednisolone/prednisone product is missing from workbook
New pediatric row; eye protection supported, but pediatric steroid dose should stay manual review
New disease row; workbook has Docusate Earwax/Dewax in Final approved
New pediatric row; guideline target patient is >6 months, avoid self-treatment if red flags
Add lookup row because product exists in Final approved but not Drug_Master_Lookup
Add lookup row because product exists in Final approved but not Drug_Master_Lookup
Add oral steroid product/BDS before enabling complete RX NOW
Do not fabricate pediatric steroid dose
New adult urgent ENT row; source supports corticosteroid option within 2 weeks but workbook lacks oral prednisone/prednisolone product
Add aliases: sudden hearing loss, hearing loss one ear, acute deafness, tinnitus sudden, etc.
Add oral prednisone/prednisolone product/BDS/price before enabling complete RX NOW
This uploaded guideline is adult-focused; do not create pediatric dosing from this source
Block routine antivirals, vasodilators, vasoactive drugs, thrombolytics for SSNHL
Main adult Ménière’s disease row; maintenance + acute attack PRN + ENT pathway
Acute Ménière’s vertigo attack row; limited vestibular suppressant only during attack
Add aliases: meniere, recurrent vertigo with tinnitus/fullness, fluctuating hearing loss
Add aliases: meniere attack, vertigo attack with ear fullness/tinnitus
Add lookup row from Final approved; acute vestibular suppressant option
Add lookup row from Final approved; brand alternative for dimenhydrinate
Add lookup row from Final approved; maintenance diuretic option with monitoring
Add lookup row from Final approved; maintenance diuretic alternative
Diuretic class is guideline-supported option, but disease-specific exact regimen/monitoring should stay clinician-reviewed
Guideline target is age ≥18; do not create pediatric regimen from this source
Do not use chronic vestibular suppressants; use only limited course during acute attacks
General adult BPPV row; procedure-first, not drug-first
Posterior canal BPPV row; Dix-Hallpike positive; Epley/CRP first-line
Lateral/horizontal canal BPPV row; supine roll test; Gufoni/barbecue roll/referral
Add aliases for positional vertigo, vertigo when turning in bed, looking up, bending down
Add lookup row from Final approved; rescue only, not definitive BPPV therapy
Add lookup row from Final approved; brand alternative for dimenhydrinate
Do not routinely prescribe vestibular suppressants; maneuver/referral should be main treatment
Guideline target is age ≥18; no pediatric dose/regimen from this source
Block routine CT/MRI, vestibular testing, chronic betahistine/antihistamine/benzodiazepine use for straightforward BPPV
General adult tinnitus triage row; non-drug first
Persistent bothersome primary tinnitus; audiology + CBT + hearing/sound therapy pathway
Red-flag tinnitus pathway; ENT/audiology/imaging decision, not routine OPD drug
Add aliases for ringing/buzzing/ear noise/tinnitus
Add aliases for one-sided tinnitus, pulsatile tinnitus, heartbeat sound in ear
No tinnitus-specific drug should be added from this guideline
Block routine antidepressants, anticonvulsants, anxiolytics, intratympanic meds, supplements, TMS
Block routine CT/MRI unless unilateral, pulsatile, focal neurologic abnormality, or asymmetric hearing loss
This guideline is adult only; no pediatric tinnitus regimen from this source
Upgrade evidence notes: INS first-line for moderate/severe; oral 2nd-gen antihistamine for mild/intermittent; add saline; avoid routine oral decongestant; intranasal decongestant only short course.
Keep oral antihistamine + intranasal steroid; keep Iliadin only max 3 days for severe congestion; add eye symptom pathway if needed.
Nasal steroid-dominant regimen with oral antihistamine and saline; escalation to combo intranasal AH+INS/immunotherapy if uncontrolled.
Phenotype-driven NAR support; steroid trial only if inflammatory/eosinophilic pattern; ipratropium if watery rhinorrhea only if available.
Typical heartburn/regurgitation without alarm features; PPI cornerstone + alginate PRN + lifestyle.
Persistent troublesome GERD symptoms despite ≥8 weeks standard-dose PPI; check adherence/timing then step-up/switch/add alginate/H2RA; refer if persistent.
Normal reflux burden/no symptom-reflux correlation; avoid repeated PPI escalation; specialist/neuromodulator pathway.
Not routine OPD Rx; endoscopy/pathology surveillance/therapy pathway only.
Add aliases for GERD, reflux, heartburn, acid regurgitation, allergic rhinitis, hay fever, nonallergic rhinitis, chronic clear rhinorrhea.
Workbook already has PPI/alginate/rhinitis drugs. Add mapping rules only; do not invent missing intranasal azelastine/ipratropium/cromoglycate products.
Adult snoring with low OSA risk; conservative/lifestyle first, consider sleep test if uncertainty.
High-risk suspected adult OSA; STOP-Bang ≥3 or high-risk clinical features → sleep test pathway, not routine drug Rx.
Positive sleep test adult OSA without major comorbidity; conservative + PAP/OA/surgery/alternative/multimodal options.
Positive sleep test adult OSA with significant comorbidity; conservative + PAP first-line, reassess response.
OSA/snoring with nasal obstruction or rhinitis limiting PAP; use nasal treatment as adjunct, not primary OSA treatment.
Poor PAP response/intolerance/refusal; troubleshoot PAP first, then consider OA/surgery/alternatives/referral.
Add aliases for snoring, loud snoring, suspected OSA, daytime sleepiness, witnessed apnea, gasping/choking, CPAP intolerance.
Add diagnostic gate: do not treat suspected OSA with sedatives/stimulants; PAP/device/referral pathway primary.
This guideline is adult-only; do not use for pediatric OSA regimen.
Adult mild/intermittent chronic constipation; lifestyle + on-demand conventional laxative.
Adult moderate chronic constipation; lifestyle + continuous conventional laxative, optimize to lowest effective dose.
Hard/lumpy stool, straining, incomplete evacuation; fiber/PEG/lactulose/MOM options.
Constipation with suspected fecal loading/rectal stool; rectal option only if no acute abdomen/red flags.
IBS-C or constipation with abdominal discomfort improved by defecation; constipation pathway only, pain-specific IBS care not fully covered.
No response after adequate lifestyle + conventional laxatives; optimize compliance then refer/special testing.
Suspected dyssynergic defecation/obstructed defecation; laxatives often poor response; refer for anorectal tests + biofeedback.
Constipation with alarm features; diagnostic/referral gate, not routine laxative-only treatment.
Add aliases for constipation, hard stool, straining, incomplete evacuation, no stool, fecal impaction, IBS-C, refractory constipation.
Add workbook-supported laxatives from Final approved into engine lookup.
Add red-flag gate, kidney caution for magnesium, long-term stimulant caution, phosphate enema caution, new-agent manual review.
Add prucalopride/lubiprostone/elobixibat/senna as guideline-supported but currently no clear workbook product row found.
Elevated BP / suspected HT; measurement + HBPM confirmation gate.
Grade 1 HT low-risk; lifestyle first, confirm/persistent HT before drug.
Grade 1 HT with high CV risk/DM/CKD/CVD/TOD; start drug after diagnosis.
Grade 2 HT; start pharmacologic treatment after diagnosis.
Grade 3 HT ≥180/110; diagnostic gate + emergency screen.
Office high but home normal; lifestyle + follow-up, drug only if high risk/TOD.
Office normal but HBPM high; assess risk/TOD and treat if high risk.
HT + diabetes; ACEI/ARB preferred first-line class, target adjusted by age.
HT + CKD with albuminuria ≥300 mg/day or mg/g; ACEI/ARB preferred.
Uncontrolled despite 3 appropriate drugs including diuretic; check adherence/white coat/secondary causes.
Pregnancy HT safety gate; avoid ACEI/ARB/DRI; severe BP urgent care.
Add aliases for high BP, HT, grade 1–3, home BP high, pregnancy HT, resistant HT.
Add workbook-supported BP products from Final approved rows.
Add emergency gate, pregnancy block, ACEI+ARB block, NSAID caution, statin/aspirin risk rules.
Methyldopa/labetalol/nicardipine IV not clearly found in workbook; keep as manual/specialist.
Add adult screening/diagnosis gate using Thai DM 2017 criteria.
Add IFG/IGT lifestyle + follow-up gate.
Add T2DM first-line metformin/lifestyle row.
Add add-on oral therapy row after metformin failure.
Add insulin/referral safety gate for FPG >300 or A1C >11 or symptomatic severe hyperglycemia.
Add CKD renal-dose safety row for metformin and sulfonylureas.
Add hypoglycemia diagnostic and urgent-care safety row.
Add pregnancy/GDM screening and referral gate.
Add aliases for diabetes, high sugar, IFG, IGT, prediabetes, hypoglycemia, GDM.
Add workbook-supported metformin, sulfonylurea, vildagliptin rows.
Add metformin eGFR, SU CKD/elderly, insulin referral, DPP4 pancreatitis, GDM referral rules.
Add missing or non-workbook diabetes agents to manual review.
Add vaginal discharge diagnostic triage gate using pH/wet prep/PMN/STI risk logic.
Add acute VC regimen with oral/vaginal azole options.
Add recurrent VC maintenance/manual row.
Add BV regimen: metronidazole oral/vaginal, clindamycin alternative.
Add TV regimen and partner treatment/abstinence rule.
Add GC row; ceftriaxone manual because not found in workbook; cefixime fallback.
Add CT/non-gonococcal cervicitis row with doxy/azithro/amox alternatives.
Add M. genitalium sequential regimen and pregnancy/lactation warnings.
Add AV diagnostic/treatment manual row; antibiotics not default due limited consensus.
Add cytolytic vaginitis non-antibiotic sodium bicarbonate row.
Add AUB PALM-COEIN triage/investigation gate.
Add HMB stable OPD symptomatic row using workbook-supported NSAID/tranexamic flags.
Add contraception safety gate for obesity/VTE/HIV/valvular heart disease.
Add aliases for discharge, candidiasis, BV, trichomoniasis, GC, CT, AUB, HMB, contraception risk.
Add workbook-supported vaginal/STD/AUB products and safety cautions.
Add pregnancy/lactation, partner treatment, STI, PID, AUB red flag, contraception contraindication rules.
Add missing ceftriaxone/metronidazole 500/clotrimazole 200/miconazole standalone/dequalinium/contraceptive LARC notes.
Add first-line primary dysmenorrhea OPD regimen with acetaminophen + NSAID + heat/exercise advice.
Add red-flag/secondary dysmenorrhea triage row before routine analgesic-only output.
Add suspected endometriosis pain row: empirical NSAID, COC option, referral/imaging gates.
Add endometriotic cyst triage row: surgery/referral if ≥4 cm, fertility desire, uncertain diagnosis, nonresponse.
Add post-op recurrence-prevention manual row; hormone therapy >6 months if no pregnancy desire.
Add MHT decision gate; do not auto-prescribe because key MHT products absent from workbook.

Add workbook-supported analgesic/COC products relevant to dysmenorrhea/endometriosis.
Add NSAID, COC, secondary dysmenorrhea, endometrioma, fertility, and MHT contraindication gates.

Add suspected axial spondyloarthritis triage row; OPD-safe NSAID bridge + rheum referral gate.
Add known axSpA active disease row; bDMARD/tsDMARD criteria and specialist-only target therapy logic.
Add lipid screening/risk assessment row using Thai CV Risk Score and LDL-C as primary target.

Add Thai CV Risk Score ≥10% primary-prevention statin regimen.
Add diabetes lipid-prevention row with LDL targets by risk.
Add secondary ASCVD prevention row; high-intensity statin default, moderate if high-risk intolerance.
Add TG ≥500 pancreatitis-prevention row with fibrate + lifestyle gate.

Add workbook-supported atorvastatin, simvastatin, fenofibrate, gemfibrozil products.
Add axSpA biologic criteria/safety gates and dyslipidemia statin/fibrate/lab-monitor rules.
Add missing biologics/JAKi/csDMARD/ezetimibe/PCSK9 notes.
Add diagnosis-first onychomycosis row; lab confirmation required before antifungal.

Add asthma diagnostic triage row using symptoms + spirometry/PEFR confirmation gate.
Add mild asthma initial treatment row; workbook supports RABA reliever but lacks inhaled ICS controller.
Add acute asthma exacerbation triage row; ED/ER treatment gate with RABA, steroid, oxygen logic.
Add suspected malaria triage row; lab-confirmation and urgent referral gate.

Add confirmed mild pemphigus row as specialist/manual systemic therapy gate; workbook lacks core pemphigus drugs.
Add moderate/severe pemphigus row with urgent evaluation/admission/specialist gate.
Add newly detected HTN confirmation/lifestyle/monitoring row.
Add grade 1 low-risk HTN row: lifestyle first, medication if persistent after 3–6 months.
Add pharmacologic HTN row using workbook-supported amlodipine/losartan/enalapril/HCTZ options.
Add CKD/albuminuria HTN row favoring ACEI/ARB with renal/K monitoring.
Add pregnancy HTN gate; workbook lacks methyldopa/labetalol; nifedipine SR/hydralazine present but specialist/OB gate.

Add infected eczema row: topical antibiotic/steroid combo or fusidic acid only when bacterial infection signs are present.

Add CDC antibiotic gate: severe >3–4 d, persistent >10 d, or worsening after initial URI improvement; prefer amoxicillin/amox-clav; avoid macrolide default.

Reinforce viral URI/common cold symptomatic-only row; avoid routine antibiotics.

Refine GAS adult row: no viral-symptom testing; RADT/culture-confirmed antibiotic only; amoxicillin/penicillin first-line x10 days.
Add pediatric GAS row with source-supported mg/kg dosing and child RADT culture-backup rule.
Refine ABRS adult row: IDSA criteria, amox-clav preferred, adult 5–7 days, no macrolide/TMP-SMX default, saline/INCS adjunct logic.

Add diagnostic gates, antibiotic gates, resistance rules, duration rules, red-flag routing.

Add mild outpatient DFI row with strict inclusion gate; antibiotic only if clinically infected, not uninfected ulcer.
Add referral/hospital pathway row; moderate/severe should not be treated as routine fast OPD.
Add IDSA 2025 scoring gate before testing; avoid unselected RADT/culture in low-risk sore throat.

Strengthen IDSA gate: ORS-first; no empiric antibiotics unless travel/severe/immunocompromised context; loperamide only adult watery/no fever/no blood.
Align with acute watery diarrhea logic; emphasize early feeding and no routine antibiotics.
Add severe traveler diarrhea antibiotic gate; Thailand travel/Campylobacter should prefer azithromycin over fluoroquinolone when antibiotic indicated.
Strengthen pediatric gate: ORS, continue feeding/breastfeeding, no loperamide, no routine antibiotics.

Add selective antibiotic row only for ill dysentery pattern with fever/tenesmus/scant bloody stool.
Add ≥14 day pathway: parasite workup, C. difficile if antibiotic exposure, consider IBD/IBS; avoid empiric antibiotics.
Add bloody diarrhea, dysentery, STEC, persistent diarrhea, traveler diarrhea severe aliases.
Add ORS rule, antibiotic gates, loperamide gate, stool testing gate, STEC blocker, peds no-loperamide, persistent diarrhea workup.

Add evaluation/referral pathway; no routine GP glaucoma drops; ophthalmology diagnosis/monitoring/treatment decision.
Add high IOP / ocular hypertension pathway; risk stratification + ophthalmology follow-up.
Add urgent red-flag corneal infection row; avoid routine conjunctivitis treatment if pain/photophobia/vision loss/corneal opacity/contact lens risk.
Add contact lens/trauma abrasion prophylaxis gate using workbook-supported topical antibiotics; no patch.
Add keratitis exclusion gate and contact-lens red-eye blocker.
Add keratitis exclusion gate and urgent referral triggers.
Add missing ophthalmic antibiotic products from Final approved into lookup for eye infection/abrasion pathways.
items.Source_File: Workbook Final approved + AAO Bacterial Keratitis PPP 2023

items.Patch_Note: Add glaucoma suspect, high IOP, corneal ulcer, keratitis, contact lens red eye, corneal abrasion aliases.
Add keratitis hard-stop, culture indication, steroid blocker, contact-lens abrasion prophylaxis, POAG suspect evaluation/follow-up rules.
Add gaps: ophthalmology referral workflow, fluorescein/slit-lamp dependency, intensive keratitis dosing, glaucoma drugs absent from workbook.
Add classic GERD adult default: 8-week PPI trial + alginate/antacid PRN + lifestyle.
Add nocturnal GERD pathway: PPI timing + avoid late meals + head elevation + alginate.
Add alarm/refractory GERD referral/endoscopy pathway.
Add source-verified treatment pathway but block full auto-RX unless optimized regimen components are available.
Add dyspepsia test-and-treat rule for <60 without alarm; high-risk lower age threshold 45–50.
Promote key GI products from Final approved into lookup for GERD/H. pylori rules.
items.Source_File: Workbook Final approved + ACG GERD/H. pylori

items.Patch_Note: Add GERD, reflux, heartburn, H. pylori, dyspepsia test-and-treat aliases.
Add PPI timing, alarm endoscopy, test-of-cure, antibiotic stewardship, H. pylori component-availability blocker.
Add missing tetracycline/rifabutin/vonoprazan, BQT optimization, Thai availability, susceptibility testing, test-of-cure workflow gaps.
Update existing adult migraine row with AAN acute-treatment gates: early treatment, triptan/NSAID options, nausea pathway, medication-overuse limits, and vascular contraindications.
items.Source_File: AAN/AHS Pediatric Acute Migraine 2019 + AAN Adult Migraine Prevention

items.Patch_Note: Add pediatric acute migraine row: ibuprofen oral solution 10 mg/kg first-line, red-flag gate, nausea pathway, medication-overuse counseling.
items.Source_File: AAN/AHS Acute Migraine in Children and Adolescents 2019

items.Patch_Note: Add pediatric prevention review row: lifestyle, headache diary, mood/anxiety screen, prevention discussion only if frequent/disabling/medication-overuse.
Add adult migraine prevention review row using workbook-supported candidates; exact prevention titration remains manual-review.
Add painful diabetic neuropathy row: non-opioid class-based pathway, expectation counseling, mood/sleep assessment, no opioid/tramadol/tapentadol.
Add muscle cramp safety row: avoid routine quinine, do not auto-use muscle relaxants as cramp treatment, supportive/manual review.
Promote AAN-relevant workbook products: ibuprofen, triptans, naproxen, amitriptyline, propranolol, candesartan, gabapentin, pregabalin, capsaicin.
Add ibuprofen suspension 10 mg/kg = 0.5 mL/kg/dose shortcut using 100 mg/5 mL workbook products.
items.Source_File: AAN/AHS Acute Migraine in Children and Adolescents 2019

items.Patch_Note: Add migraine child/adult prevention, diabetic neuropathy pain, muscle cramp aliases.
Add red-flag, medication-overuse, triptan contraindication, pediatric prevention, neuropathy opioid-block, cramp quinine-block rules.
Add missing topiramate/valproate/rizatriptan/zolmitriptan NS/sumatriptan NS/duloxetine/diltiazem/vitamin B complex gaps and safety cautions.

Add menopause vasomotor review row: HRT discussion first-line, menopause-specific CBT option, fezolinetant if HRT unsuitable, avoid SSRI/SNRI/clonidine as routine first-line for vasomotor symptoms alone.
Add GSM row: vaginal oestrogen first-line if no breast cancer history; non-hormonal moisturiser/lubricant if contraindicated/preferred; breast-cancer safety gate.
Add POI review row: under 40, symptoms + elevated FSH x2 4–6 weeks apart; offer HRT or combined hormonal contraceptive unless contraindicated.
Add obesity adult review row: ask permission, BMI + waist-to-height ratio, lifestyle/behavioural first, medicine gate after behavioural intervention evaluated.
Add orlistat-specific medicine row using workbook-supported Xenical, with eligibility and stop/review gates.
Add child/young-person obesity review row: BMI centile + waist-to-height ratio age ≥5, family-based intervention, specialist referral/comorbidity gate.
Add Xenical [Orlistat 120 mg] as restricted obesity medicine candidate.
Add aliases for menopause, hot flushes, vaginal dryness after menopause, POI, obesity, BMI, waist-to-height ratio, weight loss medicine.
Add HRT uterus rule, VTE/transdermal rule, breast cancer gate, unscheduled bleeding gate, obesity permission/non-stigma rule, BMI/waist classification, obesity medicine eligibility.
Add missing product gaps: HRT, vaginal oestrogen, fezolinetant, prasterone, ospemifene, testosterone, tirzepatide, semaglutide, liraglutide.
Add ARI triage row: self-care if safe, face-to-face gate if serious illness/comorbidity/immunosuppressed, avoid remote antibiotics unless strong reason.
Add T2DM review row: HbA1c targets, structured education, diet/activity, comorbidity assessment, sick-day rules, medication review.
Add workbook-supported metformin candidate row with renal/sick-day gate.
Add symptomatic hyperglycemia row: consider insulin or sulfonylurea, urgent red-flag gate.
Add pediatric atopic eczema mild row: emollient gap + mild topical steroid step.
Add pediatric atopic eczema flare row: appropriate potency topical steroid, step-up/step-down logic, specialist gate.
Add infected eczema row: contaminated meds replacement, bacterial infection gate, herpes simplex/herpeticum gate.
Add urgent eczema herpeticum row: systemic aciclovir + same-day derm/ophthalmology if eye involvement.
Add acute migraine row: triptan + NSAID/paracetamol strategy, antiemetic gap, opioid avoidance.
Add migraine prevention row: amitriptyline/propranolol/topiramate options; topiramate pregnancy-prevention gate.
Add medication-overuse headache row: withdrawal advice and review gate.
Add workbook-supported metformin candidates from Final approved.
Add sulfonylurea candidates as restricted hyperglycemia/diabetes review options.
Add Aristocort, Antergan, Diabederm Urea, Acyvir as restricted eczema-related candidates.
Add Relpax, sumatriptan, paracetamol, Gofen, Synflex, amitriptyline, propranolol as migraine/headache candidates.
Add missing gaps: SGLT2, GLP-1, tirzepatide, insulin, topical calcineurin inhibitors, true emollients, topical eczema antibiotics, antiemetics, topiramate.
Add recurrent UTI prevention review row: behavioural advice, referral gates, vaginal estrogen/methenamine/antibiotic prophylaxis sequence.
Add trigger-based single-dose prophylaxis review row; culture/susceptibility required.
Add methenamine hippurate alternative-to-daily-antibiotic row; avoid alkalinising sachets; review schedule.
Add suspected asthma diagnostic gate: objective testing required; no confirmed asthma without supportive test.
Add asthma ≥12 chronic management row: AIR/MART concept, block SABA-only prescribing, inhaler technique/adherence gate.
Add pediatric asthma 5–11 chronic management row: low-dose ICS + SABA initial, MART/specialist step-up.
Add B12 deficiency review row with oral/injection gap and neurologic red flag gate.
Add hypothyroidism chronic review row; levothyroxine product present but lab/titration gate required.
Add thyrotoxicosis review row; antithyroid products present but specialist/lab gate required.
Add adult hearing loss assessment/referral row; no routine medication.
Add OME row: no antibiotics/decongestants/antihistamines; hearing/speech referral gate.
Add suspected pediatric diabetes urgent row; do not routine OPD prescribe.
Add OA row: exercise/weight/core advice + topical/oral NSAID options with caution.
Add T1DM chronic/safety row; insulin module required; DKA red flag gate.
Add pediatric UTI assessment row: urine test, upper UTI red flags, age/referral gates.
Add acute gout row: NSAID/colchicine/oral steroid options; renal/PUD/drug interaction gate.
Add ULT review row: allopurinol start-low titration, flare prophylaxis, renal review.
Add acute otitis media row: no/delayed/immediate antibiotic gate; amoxicillin first-line if antibiotic needed.
Add glaucoma suspected row: urgent eye referral gate; no routine OPD steroid eye drops.
Add pelvic floor dysfunction row: pelvic floor training/referral; non-drug pathway.
Add red flag, referral, antibiotic stewardship, chronic-review, and missing-product blocking rules.
Add missing product gaps: methenamine, nitrofurantoin, true asthma ICS/formoterol, B12 injection, insulin module, glaucoma drops.
Add bronchiolitis child assessment row; supportive care only; block salbutamol/antibiotics/steroids/montelukast.
Add infected eczema row; treat eczema flare first; antibiotics not routine unless systemic/widespread/local severe.
Add low back pain row; activity/exercise core; NSAID short-course only with risk gate; avoid paracetamol alone/opioids chronic.
Add sciatica row; NSAID cautious; avoid gabapentinoids/antiepileptics/benzos/opioids chronic.
Add suspected RA urgent referral row; persistent synovitis should not wait for labs.
Add RA symptom control row; NSAID + PPI only as bridge/symptom control, not DMARD replacement.
Add suspected OSAHS row; sleep-service referral/testing; no routine sedatives.
Add OSAHS + rhinitis row; nasal steroid/antihistamine option to improve CPAP tolerance.
Add insect bite/sting local allergic reaction row; antihistamine/topical anti-itch; antibiotic blocked if no infection.
Add infected insect bite gate row; if cellulitis signs, route to cellulitis pathway.
Add tinnitus assessment/referral row; hearing assessment and urgent red flag pathways.
Add tinnitus distress management row; support, audiology, CBT/referral; block betahistine for tinnitus.
Add no-antibiotic/no-salbutamol bronchiolitis, infected eczema antibiotic stewardship, low-back medicine avoidance, tinnitus betahistine block.
Add missing products and disease modules: CPAP/non-drug rows, cellulitis pathway, RA DMARD specialist lock, tinnitus audiology route.
Add localised non-bullous impetigo antimicrobial-stewardship row: hygiene + hydrogen peroxide first if available; topical fusidic acid only if unsuitable/ineffective.
Add widespread non-bullous impetigo row; topical or oral antibiotic depending extent/practicality; no oral+topical combination.
Add bullous/systemically unwell/high-risk impetigo row; oral antibiotic/referral gate, especially baby ≤1 yr.
Add reassessment row: swab after failed topical/oral, nasal swab/decolonisation for recurrent impetigo.
Add adult uninvestigated dyspepsia row: lifestyle, PPI 4 wk, H pylori test-and-treat, 2-week PPI washout before testing.
Add adult GORD/reflux-like symptom row: full-dose PPI 4–8 wk, step down/on-demand, H2RA if inadequate response.
Add severe oesophagitis row: full-dose PPI 8 wk and maintenance; review/switch/escalate or specialist advice if failure.
Update H pylori eradication logic: 7-day PPI + amoxicillin + clarithro/metronidazole; penicillin-allergy and second-line gates.
Add NSAID-associated peptic ulcer gate: stop NSAID where possible, PPI/H2RA 8 wk, retest H pylori when indicated.
Add infant physiological GOR row: reassurance/no drug if well effortless regurgitation; hard red-flag gate.
Add peds GOR/GORD feeding row: breastfeeding assessment/feeding review, thickened formula then alginate trial; no PPI for isolated regurgitation.
Add children/young people persistent heartburn/epigastric pain row: 4-week PPI/H2RA trial with specialist referral if unresolved/recurs.
Add FH suspicion/diagnosis row: cholesterol thresholds, family history, Simon Broome/DLCN, specialist DNA/cascade testing.
Add adult FH treatment row: high-intensity statin lowest acquisition cost; aim ≥50% LDL-C reduction; ezetimibe/specialist gates.
Add child FH specialist row: referral, DNA testing if at-risk, statin usually by age 10 under specialist care.
Add adult cellulitis/erysipelas row: severity check, oral antibiotic gate, mark affected area and review if no improvement/worse.
Add peds cellulitis/erysipelas gate: age/severity/systemic illness decides oral vs hospital/specialist.
Add recurrent cellulitis prevention row: manage risk factors; prophylaxis only specialist/review pathway.
Add suspected COPD row: age >35, smoking/exposure, exertional breathlessness/chronic cough/sputum; spirometry confirmation; do not diagnose on symptoms alone.
Add COPD exacerbation row: bronchodilator optimisation; antibiotics only if sputum purulent or severe/unwell; steroid/antibiotic safety gates.
Add stress urinary incontinence row: pelvic floor muscle training ≥3 months first-line; no routine duloxetine first-line.
Add urgency/OAB row: bladder training ≥6 weeks first-line; antimuscarinic only after contraindication/cognition review.
Add POP row: lifestyle, pelvic floor, pessary/referral; urgent red flags for retention/ulceration/mass.
Add acute cough/acute bronchitis row: no routine antibiotics; self-care and red-flag pneumonia/sepsis gate.
Add antibiotic/pneumonia gate for acute cough: immediate/back-up antibiotic only if high-risk or systemically very unwell; route pneumonia pathway if suspected.
Add renal/ureteric stone acute row: NSAID first-line if safe, imaging/referral gates, alpha-blocker gap if distal ureteric stone.
Add stone prevention review row: fluid advice, stone analysis/metabolic review, thiazide/citrate only selected specialist use.
Add adult acute pyelonephritis row: same-day review red flags; oral antibiotic only if stable, culture before antibiotic if possible.
Add peds acute pyelonephritis row: fever/flank pain/UTI symptoms; hospital gate for babies/systemically unwell/vomiting/dehydration.
Add lower UTI adult woman row: antibiotic stewardship, delayed/back-up option depending severity; culture gates.
Add lower UTI special-population gate: men, pregnancy, children, catheter, recurrent or systemic symptoms need separate pathway.
Add acute sore throat no-antibiotic row: most self-limiting; use FeverPAIN/Centor score; analgesia/self-care.
Add sore throat antibiotic gate: immediate/back-up antibiotic by FeverPAIN/Centor severity and risk; avoid routine antibiotic for viral features.
Add acute sinusitis no-antibiotic row: symptoms <10 days/supportive; nasal steroid only selected adults/children ≥12 with persistent symptoms.
Add acute sinusitis antibiotic gate: symptoms ≥10 days/no improvement, systemically very unwell, severe/local complication; route existing bacterial sinusitis rows.
Add mild trunk/limb psoriasis row: emollient + topical corticosteroid/vitamin D pathway; potency/site/duration gate.
Add sensitive-site psoriasis row: short mild/moderate steroid or calcineurin/vitamin D specialist gap; avoid potent steroid long-term.
Add severe/extensive psoriasis row: DLQI/BSA/functional impact referral; systemic/biologic treatments specialist lock.
Add antimicrobial stewardship gates, drug blocks, referral gates, and specialist locks from this pasted NICE bundle.

Flag missing formulary products: hydrogen peroxide 1%, mupirocin, flucloxacillin, clarithromycin/metronidazole H pylori packs, alginate, H2RA, LAMA/LABA/ICS COPD, antimuscarinic OAB, vitamin D psoriasis, tamsulosin stone passage.
Add vitamin D prevention row for specific/at-risk groups; daily supplement 10 mcg/400 IU for most groups, infant safe-intake gate.
Add testing gate: do not routinely test vitamin D unless symptoms of deficiency, very high risk, or clinical reason.
Add pediatric idiopathic constipation assessment row; diagnose clinically, exclude red flags, avoid routine imaging/endoscopy.
Add pediatric faecal impaction/disimpaction row: PEG 3350 + electrolytes first-line escalating; rectal meds only after oral failure.
Add pediatric maintenance constipation row: PEG first-line, adjust by response, stimulant add-on/substitute, avoid abrupt stop.
Add refractory pediatric constipation referral row: no response after 3 months or infant <1 no response in 4 weeks needs specialist/DRE-capable review.
Add adult IBS diagnosis row: chronic abdominal pain/discomfort + altered bowel habit; red-flag and baseline exclusion tests.
Add IBS lifestyle/diet row: regular meals, fluid/caffeine/alcohol/fizzy drink advice, soluble fibre only, low-FODMAP only expert-led.
Add IBS-C row: laxative titrated to Bristol type 4; discourage lactulose; linaclotide only after laxative failure and ≥12 months constipation.
Add IBS-D row: loperamide first-choice antimotility; titrate to Bristol type 4.
Add IBS pain/bloating row: antispasmodic PRN, probiotic trial ≥4 weeks if chosen, avoid aloe vera.
Add refractory IBS row: TCA second-line low dose; SSRI only if TCA ineffective; psychological therapy after 12 months refractory symptoms.
Add osteoporosis fragility fracture risk-assessment row using FRAX/QFracture; age/risk-factor targeting.
Add DXA/BMD gate row: measure BMD after risk assessment near threshold, before rapid bone-loss treatment, or under 40 with major risk.
Add initial male LUTS row: history, medication review, exam/DRE, frequency-volume chart, urine dip, PSA info gate; avoid routine imaging/cystoscopy initially.
Add moderate/severe voiding male LUTS row: alpha blocker after conservative management unsuitable/failed.
Add BPE progression row: 5-alpha reductase inhibitor or combo if prostate >30 g or PSA >1.4 and high progression risk.
Add male storage LUTS/OAB row: bladder training first, anticholinergic pathway, beta-3 agonist gap, avoid penile clamps/PDE5-only use.
Add male urinary retention gate: acute retention immediate catheterisation; alpha blocker before catheter removal; chronic retention specialist workup.
Add suspected gallstone disease row: LFT + ultrasound; MRCP/EUS gates for suspected CBD stones.
Add asymptomatic gallbladder stones row: reassurance/no treatment if normal gallbladder and biliary tree.
Add symptomatic gallbladder stones row: laparoscopic cholecystectomy referral; trigger avoidance until surgery.
Add acute cholecystitis/CBD stone row: early lap chole within 1 week, bile duct clearance with surgery/ERCP, stent only temporary.
Add vitamin D testing/supplement rules, peds constipation red flags, IBS medicine sequence, osteoporosis FRAX/DXA gates, male LUTS drug/surgery gates, gallstone referral gates.
Add vitamin D preventive dose shortcut rows from PH56: infant safe intake 8.5–10 mcg/day, child 1–4 yr 10 mcg/day, age ≥4 yr/pregnant/lactating 10 mcg/day.
Add restricted/gap rows for vitamin D supplement, PEG 3350+electrolytes, stimulant laxatives, linaclotide, alpha blockers, 5ARI, anticholinergic/OAB, desmopressin, gallstone procedures.
Add remaining verification items: exact Thai products/doses for vitamin D, macrogol pediatric age licensing, IBS drugs, LUTS drugs, and gallstone non-drug referral workflow.
